declaredLane: rule harness Codex CLI subscription agent runner
observedMutationClass: Rule harness agentRunner subscription-auth route was implicit in config/runtime checks, making it easy to confuse with legacy API-key LLM diagnose.
acceptanceEvidenceClass: Codex CLI login status and rules lint; no gameplay, Unity runtime, or WebGL product acceptance evidence
rootCause: The README described the Codex CLI route as canonical but the executable agentRunner path did not require ChatGPT subscription login before invoking Codex, while legacy API-key environment variables were documented elsewhere for combined harness LLM diagnose.
prevention: Require an explicit ChatGPT subscription login preflight before Codex CLI agent work, keep API-key variables scoped to legacy LLM diagnose/API-key experiments, keep aggressive cleanup auto-apply limited to high-confidence delete_unused and simplify_inline batches, and preserve existing hard-fail coverage for active plan artifact owner shape drift, active plan concrete artifact owner collision, and progress evidence overload.
verification: Ran cmd /c codex login status and observed Logged in using ChatGPT; ran tools/rule-harness/tests/Run-RuleHarnessTests.ps1; ran run-harness-pipeline.ps1 -DryRun; ran npm run --silent rules:lint.
